characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: The patriarch of the family. A psychiatrist who works from home, married to Maggie. Ditzy Genius: Downplayed. He is a smart psychiatrist but occasionally messes up with his children, and can be goofy. Good Is Not Soft: A loving and understanding father, however he will discipline his children when they need it. Happily Married: He and Maggie have some disagreements at times, but they are usually happy together. Househusband: He runs his psychiatric practice out of his home office so he could be a stay-at-home dad to his kids while his wife works.

characters.name: Jason Seaver
characters.description: The patriarch of the family. A psychiatrist who works from home, married to Maggie. Ditzy Genius: Downplayed. He is a smart psychiatrist but occasionally messes up with his children, and can be goofy. Good Is Not Soft: A loving and understanding father, however he will discipline his children when they need it. Happily Married: He and Maggie have some disagreements at times, but they are usually happy together. Househusband: He runs his psychiatric practice out of his home office so he could be a stay-at-home dad to his kids while his wife works.

characters.name: Maggie Seaver (née Malone)
characters.description: A reporter and the matriarch of the family. The Ace: She is beautiful, smart, hard-working, good at her job, and an attentive parent at the same time. Family Versus Career: Formerly a stay-at-home mother, but at the start of the series, she goes back to work as a journalist. It was hard for her to leave her husband to take care of the kids at home, but she still tries to focus on her career while being a good mother at the same time. Happily Married: She and Jason have some disagreements at times, but they are usually happy together. Only Sane Woman: Not always, but she usually tries to be grounded arond her husband and especially her children. But she occasionally snaps.

characters.name: Mike Seaver
characters.description: The oldest child of the Seavers. Bratty Teenage Son: In early seasons, he is a scheming teenage boy who usually gets himself in trouble. Book Dumb: Despite being clever and witty, Mike doesn't care much about schoolwork, and his grades are not very good. Although he eventually becomes a teacher in later episodes. Breakout Character: Early on the show, he quickly became the fan favorite and the face of the series. He eventually started getting more focus than his parents. Characterization Marches On: He goes from a clever, charming slacker with a heart of gold in early episodes, to a responsible adult teacher who looks after Luke in season 7.

characters.name: Carol Seaver
characters.description: The smart daughter of the Seaver family. Back for the Finale: She returns for the series finale after spending part of the final season in London. Book Smart: Unlike her brothers, she is bookish and a honor student. Butt-Monkey: Mike often makes fun of her for being a nerd. Also, in season 4, both Mike and Ben joked about Carol's weight repeatedly, which infamously didn't go over well in real life. Character Development: Quite awkward in early episodes, but she eventually becomes more confident and sociable.

characters.name: Ben Seaver
characters.description: The youngest Seaver son. Annoying Younger Sibling: Mostly to Carol. He even teases her along with Mike sometimes. Big Brother Worship: Mike is his role model, and Ben often emulates his behavior. Bratty Half-Pint: In early episodes, he can be an annoying brat, due to being the youngest in the family and seeking for attention. Dumbass Teenage Son: In later episodes, he becomes a goofy and shallow teenager similar to early Mike. By the final season, theOlder and WiserMike is the mature one compared to him.

characters.name: Chrissy Seaver
characters.description: The youngest Seaver daughter, born in season 4. Adorably Precocious Child: The typical child character in a sitcom, who is smart for her age, but cute and innocent at the same time. The Baby of the Bunch: The youngest Seaver child, introduced after Ben outgrew theCheerful Childrole. Cousin Oliver: Born in Season 4 to have a new precocious character in the family dynamic. Soap Opera Rapid Aging Syndrome: Chrissy somehow manages to go from newborn to kindergarten in less than two years.

characters.name: Luke Brower
characters.description: A homeless teen who lived with the Seaver family in season 7. Cousin Oliver: It's said that the new adopted boy Luke wasn't just there to renew interest, but to prepare him to be main character in case Kirk Cameron (Mike's actor, whose relationship with the cast and crew had soured at that point), suddenly decided to walk off. The ratings didn't improve, and the seventh season would be the show's last. Dark and Troubled Past: His alcoholic stepfather used to beat him after his mother died, which is why he ran away from home. Happily Adopted: Temporarily by the Seaver family in the final season. Put on a Bus: Luke decides to go live with his father a few episodes before the series finale.
